data.title: CleanCore Solutions (ZONE) sees record revenue in Q4 and FY 2025. Q4 US revenue tops $1.1M, FY revenue up 29% to $2.1M.
data.description: Revenue from the United States in the fourth quarter surpassed $1.1 million, marking a significant increase. The total revenue for the full year rose by 29% to reach $2.1 million. These figures showcase strong growth and financial performance for the specified time periods.

data.title: Horizon Aircraft reveals Q4 and full year 2025 performance in latest business update.
data.description: A recent report indicates a significant increase in unemployment rates, with the current figure standing at 6.9%. This marks a stark rise of 2.4% from the previous month. In parallel, the number of new jobless claims has surged to 900,000, surpassing expectations and reflecting the continued economic challenges facing the nation due to the ongoing pandemic. Furthermore, the report highlights a concerning trend as industries struggle to recover and reimagine their workforce amidst the uncertain environment, underscoring the need for targeted support and interventions to stabilize the labor market.

data.title: Hofseth Biocare ASA's Q2 2025 financial report: NOK 68.4M total operating revenue, down from NOK 80.4M same period last year.
data.description: In the second quarter of 2025, HBC reported a total operating revenue of NOK 68.4 million, a decrease from NOK 80.4 million in the corresponding period of 2024. The net operating revenue for the quarter was NOK 67.8 million, down from NOK 72.2 million in the same quarter of the previous year. The total operating revenue in the first half of 2025 amounted to NOK 129.3 million, slightly lower than the NOK 132.8 million generated in the same period in 2024.
Honkarakenne Oyj's half-year report for Jan 1 - Jun 30, 2025, released. Check it out for detailed financial info.

data.title: Bavarian Nordic reports first half 2025 results in Copenhagen, announces financial calendar for 2026.
data.description: Bavarian Nordic A/S (OMX: BAVA) revealed its interim financial results and business advancements for the initial half of 2025 in Copenhagen, Denmark, on August 22. The company also disclosed its financial schedule for 2026.
Prosafe SE's latest report reveals Q2 earnings and first half of 2025 performance data.
Stockwik releases Q2 2025 report covering January 1 - June 30, revealing key financial figures.

data.title: Dow Jones closes nearly flat. Nasdaq hammered, Palantir and Nvidia big losers. Manufacturing services name sold off.
data.description: On Tuesday, the Dow Jones index ended the day with a small gain, while tech giants Nvidia and Palantir experienced declines. Additionally, a manufacturing services company saw a significant drop in its stock price. The overall market performance saw the Dow Jones nearly unchanged, but the Nasdaq index faced substantial losses. This information was shared in a recent market update with Nvidia and Palantir highlighted as major losers of the day by a leading financial publication.

data.title: Cytokinetics grants inducement stock options to 46 employees: 105,169 shares of common stock and 70,879 RSUs.
data.description: On August 15, 2025, Cytokinetics, Incorporated (Nasdaq: CYTK) based in South San Francisco, granted stock options for 105,169 shares of common stock and 70,879 restricted stock units (RSUs) to 46 employees. The stock options were given as a material inducement for their employment that began in July and August 2025. The RSUs will be converted into common stock upon vesting.

data.title: Wall Street traders prepare for potential tech meltdown as QQQ options indicate a sudden plunge, with put skew nearing 3-year highs.
data.description: Wall Street traders are increasing their bets on a significant tech meltdown by purchasing deep out-of-the-money puts on the Invesco QQQ Trust, which has risen 43% since April. This surge in bearish options signals concerns about the market's rally facing potential risks. The put skew, a measure of how much investors are willing to pay for crash insurance, is nearing three-year highs for the QQQ ETF, indicating a heightened fear of extreme tech declines. Institutional investors are monitoring this trend closely to hedge gains from the tech stock surge. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Walmart stock (WMT) fell after first earnings miss in 3 years. CEO warns tariffs increasing costs, impacting bottom line.
data.description: Shares of Walmart, Inc. (NYSE: WMT) dipped following its first earnings miss in three years. CEO Doug McMillon's warnings about the negative impact of tariffs added pressure to the stock. McMillon stated during the Q2 earnings call that the company is experiencing repercussions from President Donald Trump's tariffs on its profits. The stock is currently slipping, with the latest real-time price available. President Trump's net worth is now over $5.5 billion after investing $100 million in bonds during his time in office. For more details, refer to the complete story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: After Russia's invasion of Ukraine in 2018, commodity prices have reset. GLD ETF is at record highs. In Feb 2022, DBC rallied 15.1%, outperforming SPY by 65% from Dec 2021-Jun 2022. Oil spiked to $130/barrel. Energy panic of 2022 is now over.
data.description: Three and a half years after Russia's invasion of Ukraine shook global markets, commodity prices have mostly stabilized. In late February 2022, as the war began, prices for goods like oil and wheat surged. The Invesco DB Commodity Index Tracking Fund (NYSE: DBC) spiked by 15.1% in a week, its biggest move since inception in 2006. Between December 2021 and June 2022, DBC outperformed the U.S. stock market each month, with a 65% lead. The post-pandemic global economy, with soaring demand, stimulus, and limited supply, saw prices climb further due to the war in Ukraine. However, high commodity prices often self-correct. For instance, crude oil hit $130 per barrel in March 2022 but has since eased. The full article can be viewed on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Cathie Wood's ARKK is outshining Big Tech, with PLTR shares up over 100% YTD. ARKK has beaten the Nasdaq 100 by 30% in the last five months.
data.description: Five years after gaining retail popularity with a remarkable rally, Cathie Wood's ARK Innovation ETF (CBOE: ARKK) is once again surpassing Big Tech in performance. Unlike the speculative surge driven by low rates and stimulus in 2020, this current rally appears to be more stable. PLTR shares have surged over 100% year-to-date. From March 2020 to February 2021, ARKK skyrocketed by 360%, far outpacing the Invesco QQQ Trust (NASDAQ: QQQ), which rose by 100%. As of summer 2025, ARKK has outperformed the Nasdaq 100 by 30% in the last five months, the strongest streak since January 2021. The focus has shifted from future profit expectations to actual performance of technology platforms. The reasons behind ARKK’s recent rally are explored in the full story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Approach health goals diligently for success. Follow through is key for achieving goals.
data.description: Achieving your health goals is not just about setting them, but also about what comes after. Persistence and determination to follow through are crucial for success. With a diligent and driven mindset, you are on the right path towards achieving your health goals. Remember to approach them with dedication and commitment for optimal results.

data.title: Beta-glucan market to hit $800 million by 2029, driven by diverse applications in various industries, led by North America.
data.description: The global beta-glucan market size is projected to increase from $542 million in 2024 to $800 million by 2029, at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 8.1%. This growth is attributed to the rising demand for natural food additives, increased use in pharmaceuticals, and its emergence in cosmetics and sustainable packaging, like eco-friendly food packaging. Beta-glucan, known for its health benefits like boosting immunity and lowering cholesterol, is now being explored in various industries for innovative applications. North America leads the market, with key players such as Merck and Lesaffre. The region utilizes beta-glucan in health-focused products, as a thickening agent, fat substitute, dietary fiber, and hypocholesterolemic agent in response to consumer trends favoring healthier, natural ingredients. This growth signifies a shift towards sustainable and health-promoting ingredients in various sectors.

data.title: Arthur Hayes of BitMEX challenges Powell with 'no cut' stance at Jackson Hole, calls for unconventional measures; references Volcker.
data.description: BitMEX co-founder Arthur Hayes sparked controversy before Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's Jackson Hole speech, advocating against an interest rate cut to push President Trump towards unconventional fiscal actions. Hayes questioned whether Powell would yield to Trump or follow a more aggressive path like former Chair Paul Volcker, who raised rates to 21.5% in the 1980s to tackle inflation. By resisting political pressures, Powell could prompt Trump to resort to money printing or quantitative easing. The scenario raised by Hayes sets the stage for a potential showdown between the Fed Chair and the President. Hayes' insights and predictions can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Tech stocks on Nasdaq 100 set for 3rd day loss, as Walmart sinks; Jobless claims increase to 235k in economic update.
data.description: Tech stocks on the Nasdaq 100 were set for a third day of losses as investors took a cautious approach before Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's speech at the annual Jackson Hole Symposium. Palantir Technologies Inc. (NASDAQ: PLTR) traded flat in New York, following six days of declines wiping out 18% from recent highs. Major indexes like the S&P 500, Dow Jones, and Nasdaq Composite were all down around 0.4%, with only a few sectors in the green. Weekly jobless claims rose to 235,000, the highest in 8 weeks, indicating a cooling labor market. However, flash PMIs from S&P Global showed a stronger rebound than expected in manufacturing and services activity for August, signaling strong business sentiment. The U.S. dollar index (DXY) was... (Full story available on Benzinga.com)

data.title: Global surgical sutures market expected to reach USD 6.65 billion by 2030, growing at a CAGR of 6.5% from 2025 to 2030. Aging population and chronic conditions drive demand. Technological advancements like absorbable sutures and antibacterial coatings fuel market growth. Low- and middle-income countries offer untapped potential.
data.description: The global surgical sutures market was valued at US$4.56 billion in 2024, reaching US$4.84 billion in 2025. It is projected to grow at a CAGR of 6.5% from 2025 to 2030, with an expected valuation of US$6.65 billion by 2030. This growth is driven by increased demand for surgical procedures globally, advancements in suture design, and improved access to surgical care in emerging economies. The aging population and the prevalence of chronic conditions are contributing to a rise in surgical interventions, along with trauma and accidental injuries. Technological innovations such as absorbable sutures, antibacterial coatings, and barbed designs are improving infection control and healing outcomes. Challenges include the shift towards minimally invasive surgeries, while opportunities lie in low- and middle-income countries with increasing healthcare investments. Markets in North America and Europe face pricing pressures and competition.

data.title: Trump's 'Bela' post sparks meme token rally, Circle unveils new blockchain for stablecoins, Tom Lee bets $5 billion on Ethereum.
data.description: In the last week, significant events have occurred in the financial and cryptocurrency sectors. Circle Internet Group Inc introduced a new blockchain named Arc catering to stablecoin finance. They also unveiled Circle Gateway for instant cross-chain liquidity. However, the company's shares faced a downturn at the beginning of the week. Additionally, Tom Lee, Fundstrat co-founder, made a substantial $5 billion bet on Ethereum. Further details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: A new study found that 65% of people prefer coffee over tea, while 35% prefer tea.
data.description: Recently, there has been a surge in job growth with approximately 600,000 new jobs being added to the workforce. The unemployment rate has also dropped to 4%, the lowest it has been in the past two years. Wages have seen a significant increase as well, with average hourly earnings rising by 3.5%. This positive trend in the job market has been attributed to various factors, including increased consumer spending and a boost in confidence among employers. Overall, the current economic outlook appears to be promising with these latest statistics reflecting a strong and growing job market.
